summary: Matched five previously undecompiled ftKirby special-N-related functions across Kirby copy-ability files: EatWalk IASA, Link-copy air end animation, two motion-state predicate helpers, and a Yoshi-copy grab/timer helper. The PR reports clean ninja verification and 100% fuzzy match for all five functions.
what_changed: Implemented ftKb_EatWalk_IASA in ftKb_SpecialN.c, handling jump input from Kirby's eat-walk state, motion changes to ftKb_MS_EatJump1 or ftKb_MS_EatWait, shared SpecialN setup calls, and walk common fallback via ftWalkCommon_800DFEC8.

Implemented ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim in ftKb_SpecialNPk.c, unsetting Link-copy arrow and bow state when animation ends, then selecting fall or controlled freefall behavior based on Link/Young Link copy dat_attrs toggles.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C as a bool helper that returns false only for a specific 0x197-0x19C motion-id range when fp->x2070.x2071_b6 is not set; NULL and default cases return true.
Implemented fn_8010B16C in ftKb_SpecialNYs.c, adjusting grab_timer by percentTemp-scaled damage logic, forcing it to zero under a bury-related condition, and setting fp->x1828 to 4.
Added ftCo_Bury.h include in ftKb_SpecialNYs.c to support the newly decompiled bury-related helper call.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C in ftKb_SpecialNZd.c as a Mewtwo-copy special-N motion-state predicate, checking ground and air start/loop/full/cancel/end motion IDs and fp->x2070.x2071_b6.
Replaced five placeholder decomp markers with C implementations that the PR summary says match at 100% fuzzy_match_percent.
smart_moves: Preserved matching-sensitive stack behavior with PAD_STACK in ftKb_EatWalk_IASA and ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim.
Used #pragma dont_inline around fn_8010B16C, indicating inlining had to be explicitly controlled to match the original codegen.
Kept the odd-looking control flow in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C, including a goto end_true for the NULL path, rather than simplifying it away and risking a mismatch.
Encoded motion-state predicate helpers as switch statements with explicit cases, matching the apparent original branching structure for copy-ability state checks.
Used dat_attrs fields for Link and Young Link freefall toggles instead of hardcoding behavior, preserving character-specific copy-ability data flow.
decomp_lessons: For ftKirby copy-ability predicates, NULL gobj paths may intentionally return true; do not assume NULL should be false without evidence from the target function.
Kirby copied-special helpers often gate behavior on fp->x2070.x2071_b6 only within specific motion-state ranges, while default motion states return true.
Matching IASA functions may require temporary variables such as var in ftKb_EatWalk_IASA even when the logic could be simplified, because the original control flow matters.
Animation-end handlers should mirror cleanup order before state transition; ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim unsets arrow and bow before entering fall/freefall.
When decompiling timer/damage helpers, preserve field casts and comparisons such as (s32) fp->dmg.x18CC == 3 because these can be codegen-significant.
Headers should be added narrowly when a matched function introduces a dependency; this PR added ftCo_Bury.h only where the bury-related call was needed.
naming_conventions: ftKb_* prefix is used for Kirby fighter-specific functions.

Copy-ability file suffixes indicate copied special-N sources, such as SpecialNPk for Link/Pichu/Pikachu-related grouping in the file name context, SpecialNYs for Yoshi, and SpecialNZd for Mewtwo/Zelda-related grouping in the file name context; exact grouping beyond changed filenames is not established by the excerpt.
Motion-state constants use ftKb_MS_* names for Kirby motion states, for example ftKb_MS_EatJump1, ftKb_MS_EatWait, and ftKb_MS_MtSpecialNLoopFull.
Unidentified or local helper functions remain with address-style names such as fn_8010B16C, ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C, and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C when stronger semantic names are not evident.
Fighter field names such as fp->x2070.x2071_b6, fp->dmg.x1838_percentTemp, and fp->grab_timer were reused as existing struct names rather than renamed in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma dont_inline on/reset was used around fn_8010B16C to preserve non-inlined code shape.
A goto label end_true was retained in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C for matching-sensitive NULL/default return behavior.
Switch-case motion-id classification was used for predicate helpers instead of condensed range checks in the Mewtwo-copy function, likely to match original branching.
Boolean helpers explicitly return true/false rather than returning the flag expression directly, preserving original-style control flow.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body reports verification with ninja and fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0 for all functions.
follow_up_queries: Search for other ftKirby copy-ability predicate helpers that check fp->x2070.x2071_b6 inside motion-state switches.
Compare ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C against similar copied special-N helpers for naming or abstraction opportunities.
Look for additional fn_8010B16C callsites to clarify whether the helper should eventually receive a semantic name tied to Yoshi-copy swallow, bury, or grab timer behavior.
Check whether ftKb_LkSpecialNEnd_Anim and ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim share cleanup/freefall patterns useful for future Link-copy matches.
